page: Amazon Web Services trn2.3xlarge is an instance within the trn2 server family, built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel CPU with 12 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) derived from 6 physical cores, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 10.67 GB of RAM per physical core.

For local storage, the trn2.3xlarge includes a bundled 470 GB NVMe SSD. The network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Although designated as an AWS Trainium Gen2 3xlarge instance, the hardware profile reports a GPU count of 0.0, indicating that standard graphics processing units are not present in this specific slice.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides resource density suitable for memory-intensive operations without the overhead of excess compute cores. However, the lack of standard GPUs limits its utility for traditional graphics-accelerated tasks. This configuration is engineered for workloads such as database hosting, memory-intensive data processing, and network-heavy distributed applications.
description: Amazon Web Services trn2.3xlarge is an x86_64 server within the trn2 family, utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated Intel vCPUs (6 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 470 GB NVMe SSD. With a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps and a GPU count of 0.0, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 10.67 GB per core. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and network-heavy applications.

tagline: A memory-dense x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel processors, fast NVMe local storage, and high-speed baseline networking.

128.0 GB of system memory (10.67 GB per core)
470 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture
Suited for memory-intensive data processing and database hosting
